macro_segments.title: Introduction and Overview of Satisfying Conditions (Tna'im) in Gittin

micro_segments.title: Opening remarks on satisfying conditions and the cloak case
Discussion of waiving conditions versus treating as satisfied through hana'ah
Comparison to nedarim case and introduction of tnai formulation issues

macro_segments.title: The Sharecropper Case: Substituting the Required Action When Circumstances Change

micro_segments.title: Rav Yosef and Rava's dispute on whether rain substitutes for required irrigation
Comparison to Rabban Shimon ben Gamliel's ruling on money substituting for a cloak
Distinction between gittin and other transactions: why conditions in gittin must be precise

macro_segments.title: Hillel's Takkana: Giving Money Against One's Will in the Context of Yovel

micro_segments.title: Original practice of hiding on the last day of the year to prevent buyback
Hillel's institution allowing money to be deposited and doors broken without consent
Rava's inference: whether giving money against someone's will counts as giving
Rav Pappa's counter-position: Hillel's takana may only address absence, not unwillingness
Resolution: ambiguity remains on whether nesinat ba'al korcha works

macro_segments.title: Halacha According to Rabban Shimon ben Gamliel and the Paperless Get Case

micro_segments.title: Rule: we pasken like Rabban Shimon ben Gamliel in our Mishna except in two cases
Baraita: if he keeps the paper, she is not divorced; if condition is to return it, she is
Rav Chisda: explaining via Rabban Shimon ben Gamliel's position on substitutes
Abaye's challenge: Rabban Shimon only allows substitutes when the object is missing

macro_segments.title: Formal Rules of Tna'im (Mishpatei Hatna'im): Doubling, Order, and Internal Consistency

micro_segments.title: Abaye's solution: the tnai is invalid because Rabbi Meir requires doubling the condition
Rava's objection: if doubling were required, the tnai would be binding, making the get invalid
Alternative grounds: tnai must precede maaseh; here maaseh precedes tnai
Rav Adda's refinement: tnai and maaseh must be in different domains, not the same thing

macro_segments.title: Tosafot's Framework: Three Categories of Tna'im Based on Da'at and Formality

micro_segments.title: Tosafot's reading: all Mishna cases require formal tnai kaful, consistent with Rabbi Meir
Three categories: formal tna'im, giluy da'at, and dvrim shebilev kol adam
Application: when external conditions are fundamental to intent versus peripheral demands

macro_segments.title: Shmuel's Takkana for Shachiv Meira: Ordering the Condition Correctly

micro_segments.title: Shmuel requires stating condition negatively first to avoid ayin hara (evil eye)
Requirement of hen kodem l'lav: affirmative before negative in the condition

macro_segments.title: The Nursing and Service Conditions: Implicit Duration and Non-Performance

micro_segments.title: Mishna: serve father and nurse child; implicit duration of two years unless stated otherwise
If child or father dies before service begins, get is still valid (Tosafot reading)
When time is explicit, the full period must be served; if not her fault, get is valid
Rabban Shimon ben Gamliel's principle: any withholding not her fault validates the get

macro_segments.title: Dispute Between Rabbanan and Rabban Shimon on Interpreting Conditions Liberally

micro_segments.title: Baraita: nursing for one day satisfies the condition; contradiction with Mishna's two-year requirement
Rav Chisda: Mishna is Rabban Shimon ben Gamliel (lenient); Baraita is Rabbanan (strict)
Gemara's refinement: distinction based on liberal interpretation of satisying conditions versus literal reading

topical_tags.term: Nesinat ba'al korcha (giving against one's will)
Hen kodem l'lav (yes before no in formulation)
